title: rubus celticus
intro: Rubus celticus, also known as the wild Irish raspberry, is a type of bramble plant that is native to Ireland and other parts of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content. The health benefits of Rubus celticus include its ability to help lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
